assertions.text: The vehicle accelerated for about ten seconds after pulling away.
The vehicle reached roughly 45 km/h prior to the impact.
The impact occurred after the vehicle had been accelerating for about ten seconds.
After the collision, the vehicle slowed and rolled to a near stop.
The vehicle had just pulled away immediately before accelerating.
There was no additional impact after the single heavy impact (only one impact total).

verdicts.evidence: The GPS speed series shows speed rising from [0.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) to a maximum of [47.0 km/h](T3) over the window, and the accelerometer sustained-accel detection spans about [10.41 s to 11.27 s](T2) indicating roughly [~10 s] of acceleration prior to impact at [11.01 s](T2).
GPS speed maximum is recorded as [47.0 km/h](T3) and a speed-drop detection reports a pre-impact speed of [45.0 km/h](T2) before the rapid reduction to [22.0 km/h](T2).

Detect_events found two impact_spike detections coincident at [11.01 s](T2) on accel_x (peak at [11.01 s](T2)) and accel_y (peak at [11.01 s](T2)), indicating a single impact time rather than multiple separate impacts.

The accel_x channel shows a peak signed deviation of [−5.862 g] at [11.01 s](T3) and accel_y a peak of [−2.665 g] at [11.01 s](T3), which exceed the impact-spike threshold and indicate a heavy impact.
The sustained acceleration segment is reported from [10.41 s to 11.27 s](T2) and the impact peak is at [11.01 s](T2), consistent with the impact occurring after about [~10 s] of acceleration.

GPS speed shows a drop from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s](T2) and the overall gps_speed last value is [2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s](T3), indicating the vehicle slowed and rolled toward a near stop after the collision.

There is no explicit channel indicating an ignition or gear change; GPS shows first_value [0.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) and then acceleration, but 'pulled away immediately before accelerating' (timing/driver action) cannot be determined from sensors alone.

Only one impact time is detected: impact spikes on accel_x and accel_y are both at [11.01 s](T2) with no additional impact_spike detections elsewhere in the window (detect_events returned [n_detections 5](T2) and listed the two coincident spikes), supporting that there was a single impact.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single, strong impact at [11.01 s](T2) with high peak accelerations ([−5.862 g](T3), [−2.665 g](T3)), GPS speed reaching [47.0 km/h](T3) and dropping from [45.0 to 22.0 km/h](T2) after impact, and final speed near [2.0 km/h at t=16.0 s](T3). These data support the narrative's main assertions about acceleration, pre-impact speed, single heavy impact, and slowing to near stop; the 'pulled away immediately before' claim is not directly observable.

citations.claim: 45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0 s

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned
